content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
september 25, L99o
clinton Pridmore
President
Napa County Resource conservation District
ceneral Engineering contractors
1305 capelI valley Road
Napa, cA 94558
Re3 Your Request for Infornal Assistance
our File No. r-9o-519
Dear l,!r . Pridmore :
This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
of telephone advice provided to you on July 31, 1990, regarding
your respons ibitities 
under the confl ict-of-interest 
provisions of
trre potiiical 
Reform Act (the 'Act").1 
Your request for advice is
general in nature and does not concern a specific decision before
your agency. consequently, we treat your letter as a request for
informal assistance pursuant to the provisions of Regulation
18329 (c) (copy enclosed) . z
OUESTION
Do the confl ict-of- interest provisions of the
frorn working as the grading contractor in a parcel
sion control regulation by the Napa valley Resource
District of which you are president?
Act prevent you
subject to ero-
conservat ion
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prevent you from working as the grading
contractor in a parcel subject to erosion control regulation by
the Napa Valley Resource conservation District. 
However, in your
official- capaci,ty as president of the district, 
you must abstain
1 corr".rr."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. Al-1 statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
Comrnission
regulations appear at 2 californj.a code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All- references to reguJ.ations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
2 Informal assj,stance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Sec-
tion 83L14; Regulation 18329 (c) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacrarnento CA 958O4-O8O? o (916) 3 22.5660


File No. r-9o-519
Page 2
fron participating in decisions which will have a material
financial effect on you, or on your business, or on any source of
incoroe of $250 or more in the 12 nonths preceding the decision.
AdditionaLly, the rrnexusrr provisions of the Act require you to
disqualify yourself from partj"cipating in decisions regarding ero-
sion control in the particular parcel.
FACTS
on July 31, 1990, you telephoned this office for advice and
inforned ne that you are the elected Presj-dent of the Napa valIey
Resource Conservation District (the ttdistricttt). 
You are also the
sole owner of an excavation and general contracting business-3
You vish to enter j,nto a contract to provide your services as a
grading contractor for the Jim silva subdivision parcel split in
capell Va11ey. Your work will involve winterizing the land to
niiriroize damlge from erosj-on. tlork performed on the parcel is
subject to approval by the district.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official 
frorn making,
participating in naking, or otherwi.se using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in $rhich the of-
ticiat has a financial interest. 
As president of the Napa county
Resource conservation District you are a public official- 
(Sec-
tion 82048. )
Section 87103 specifies that an official 
has a financial
interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 8710o if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a rnaterial
financial efiect, distinguishable from the effect on the public
generally, on the official 
or a mernber of his or her immediate
fanily or on:
(a) Any
official 
has
one thousand
3 Th""" facts include
teLephone conversation
business entity in
a direct or indirect
dollars ($1,000) or
which the public
investrnent worth
more .
***
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a comrnercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dol-
lars ($250) or more in value provided to, received
by or promised to the public official- within 12
months prior to the tine when the decision is made.
information you provided to me during our
of August 24, L99O.


Fi■e No. ■-90-5■9
Page 3
(d) Any bus j,ness
official 
is a director,
employee, or holds any
entity in which the public
officer, partner, trustee,
position of management.
***
For purposes of this sectj,on, indirect invest-
ment or j.nterest means any investment or interest
owned by the spouse or dependent child of a public
official, 
by an agent on behaff of a public of-
ficial, 
or by a business entity or trust in which
the official, 
the official's 
agents, spouse, and
dependent children own directly, indirectly, 
or
beneficially a 1o-percent interest or greater.
You are the sole osrner of an excavating and contracting busi-
ness. For purposes of our discussion $re assume that your interest
in your company exceeds $1,000. Under these facts, you rnust
ilisqualify yourself frorn participating in any decision which would
foreseeably and materiall-y affect you or your business- You are
also required to disqualify yourself from participating in any
decision which would foreseeably and materially affect any person
(including a busj,ness entity) which has been a source of incorne to
you in excess of $250 in the 12 months preceding the decision, or
lffect any business entity in which you are a director, officer,
partner, trustee, employee, or hold any position of management.
-oisqualif ication is required whenever the decision will affect
theJe interests in a manner that is distinguishabfe from the ef-
fect on the public generally.
Foreseeab i l itv
The effects of a decisj-on are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that they will occur. To be foresee-
ab1e, the effects of a decision must be more than a rnere possibil-
ity; however certainty is not reguired. 
(Dolrnev cares v. Downev
Comnunitv Development Com. (1987) 196 Cal'. App. 3d 983, 9a9-99L;
witt v. Morrow (1977) 70 cal. APp. 3d 817; In re Thorner (1975) L
FPPC ops. 198 (copy enclosed) . ) The Act seeks to Prevent more
than attual conflicts of interest, it seeks to prevent even the
appearance of a possible conflict of interest. 
(witt v. Morro$,,
supra at 823. )
Your company is engaged in the business of erosion control.
when you cornplete an erosion control project, the district 
of
which you are president aPproves the work performed. The district
nay deiry approial and require additional work on the land. rf it
is- foreseeltle ttrat the decision will have a rnaterial financial
effect on you, on your business, or on any source of income of
9250 or moie in the preceding 12 months, disqualifj.cation is
required.


File No. I-90-519
Page 4
Mater ia l itv
To require disqualification, 
the foreseeable effect of a
decision on your financial interests must be material. 
The com-
mission has adopted regTulations which clarify when the effect of a
decision is material. 
when a source of incom-e of $25o or more in
the 12 months preceding the decision is directly involved in a
decision before the official's 
agency, the official 
must
disqualify himself or herself frorn participating in the decis.ion.
(nequtation La7o2.1(a), copy enclosed.) A source of income is
directly involved in a decision before the public official's
agency when it:
(1) Initiates the proceeding in which the
decision will be nrade by filing an applicatj-on,
c1ain, apPeal, or similar reguest or;
(2) Is a named party in, or the subject of,
the proceeding concerning the decision before the
official 
or the official's 
agency.
(3) A person or business entity is the
subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the
issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation
of any license, pernit, or other entitlement to, or
contrict with, the subject person or business
entitY.
Regnrlation 18702. 1 (b) .
Completion of the erosion control work is subject to district
approval. When the individual or business for whom you have
pliforurea erosion control work and who has been a source of income
Lo you of $250 or more in the preceding 12 months comes before the
dj.strict for approval of the work, you rnust disqualify yourself
fron participating in the decision.
The decision to approve the erosion control- work performed on
the property will also indirectly affect your business. This is
so because additional work may be reguired. when a business
entity in which a public official 
has an interest of more than
$1,ooo is indirectly involved in a decision before the official's
agency, Regutation La7o2.2 (copy enclosed) provi,des the necessary
guiaelines to deternine whether the decision is material. 
For a
ielatively snall business entity, the indirect effect of a deci-
si-on is rnaterial if :
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of $1o,000 or more; or


File No. I -9 o-519
Page 5
(2) The decision vrill result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or elirninating existing expenses for a
fiscal year in the amount of $2,500 or nore; or
(3) The decision will result in thE increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of $10,000 or nore.
Regulation L87 02 .2 (q)
Thus, if the decision to approve the erosion control vrork you have
performed will have a rnaterial financial effect on your business,
your disqualification is requi,red.
Nexus
Additionally, you are required to disqualify yourself if
there is a I'nexusrr between the purpose for which you receive
income in your private capacity and a governmental- decision. 
A
nexus situation exists if you receive j-ncorne to achieve a goal or
purpose which would be achieved, defeated, aided, or hindered by
the decision. (Regulation 7A7O2.1(d).) Upon completion of the
erosion control work, the project will come before the district
for approval of the work perforned. Under these circumstances,
there is a disqualifying nexus because you would be approving i.n
your official 
capacity that for which you received income to ac-
complish in your private capacity. oisqualification is thus
required.
We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questj,ons regarding this natter, do
not hesitate to call ne at (916) 322-5901-
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabri,n
Acting General Counsel
